title: Preserving Cultural Heritage
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Museums and ancient sites represent some of the most important (0) historical resources that societies possess. The (17) preservation of these cultural treasures requires significant funding and expertise. Buildings and artefacts of (18) considerable age must be carefully protected from the effects of time and pollution. Old city centres are particularly (19) attractive to tourists from around the world, bringing economic benefits to local communities. Many countries invest heavily in maintaining (20) traditional crafts, languages, and customs that form part of their national identity. These efforts ensure that (21) cultural practices are not lost as societies modernise and change. Museums also play an important (22) educational role, offering exhibitions that teach visitors about the past. When historic buildings are damaged, skilled craftspeople are employed to carry out sensitive (23) restoration work. The knowledge and skills passed down through generations are considered truly (24) invaluable and irreplaceable.

example.text: Museums and ancient sites represent some of the most important (0) HISTORICAL resources that societies possess. (HISTORY)

questions.explanation: A noun is needed after 'The'; 'preserve' becomes 'preservation' by replacing '-ve' with '-vation'.
An adjective is needed before 'age'; 'consider' becomes 'considerable' by adding the suffix '-able'.
An adjective is needed after 'particularly'; 'attract' becomes 'attractive' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
An adjective is needed before 'crafts'; 'tradition' becomes 'traditional' by adding the suffix '-al'.
An adjective is needed before 'practices'; 'culture' becomes 'cultural' by adding the suffix '-al'.
An adjective is needed before 'role'; 'educate' becomes 'educational' by adding the suffix '-ional'.
A noun is needed before 'work'; 'restore' becomes 'restoration' by replacing '-re' with '-ration'.
A negative adjective is needed; 'value' → 'valuable', then the prefix 'in-' creates 'invaluable', meaning 'too precious to measure'.
